zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Plumpton於大約1446年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特時，她的父親Robert Plumpton32歲，母親Mary Hill28歲。她於1462年與Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy在英國英格蘭艾可萊特爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1498年5月16日在英國英格蘭艾可萊特爾享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾希爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de Plumpton于大约1446年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特时，她的父亲Robert Plumpton32岁，母亲Mary Hill28岁。她于1462年与Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy在英国英格兰艾可萊特爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1498年5月16日在英国英格兰艾可萊特爾享年53岁，埋葬在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾希爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Plumpton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called with Old English plūme, plȳme ‘plum, plum tree’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, including Plumpton (Yorkshire, Northamptonshire, Sussex, Cumbria), Fieldplumpton or Woodplumpton (Lancashire), Plympton (Devon), and a lost Plumpton in Kingsbury (Warwicks).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Plumpton was born about 1446, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Robert Plumpton, was 32 and her mother, Mary Hill, was 28. She married Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy in 1462, in Writtle, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 16 May 1498, in Writtle, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in Hill, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane de Plumptonが約1446年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアトウセスターで生まれた時，父Robert Plumptonは32歳，母Mary Hillは28歳であった。1462年にイギリスイギリスエセックスウリットルでSir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1498年5月16日にイギリスイギリスエセックスウリットルで53歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアヒルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Plumpton(이)가 대략 1446년 영국 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Plumpton(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Hill(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1462년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀에서 Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1498년 5월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 힐에 묻혔다.

ru.St Lawrences Church, Towcester, Northamp: ст ларенсе'с чурч, тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Plumpton родилась около 1446 г. в местности тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Plumpton было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Hill, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy в 1462 г. в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 16 мая 1498 г. в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности хилл, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Easton on the Hill, East Northamptonshir: Easton on the Hill, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.St Lawrences Church, Towcester, Northamp: St Lawrence's Church, Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane de Plumpton nació aproximadamente 1446, en Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Plumpton, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mary Hill, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Empson Chancellor of the Duchy en 1462, en Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 16 de mayo de 1498, en Writtle, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en Hill, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
